title: SENTIMENTAL VALUE
logline: When actress Nora returns to her family's century-old house after her mother's death, the building becomes both witness and participant in a multi-generational saga exploring memory, family trauma, and the weight of inherited stories.
antagonistOrConflict: Family legacy and unresolved generational trauma

actBreaks.description: End of establishment - family funeral concludes, individual journeys begin

validation.ruling: Scene #74 effectively ends Act 1 - after the funeral and family tensions, Gustav approaches Nora with his film project proposition. This transitions from the setup phase into the main narrative thrust.

actBreaks.description: Discovery phase ends - Agnes uncovers family secrets at National Archives

validation.ruling: Scene #147 marks a structural transition point at 79% where Nora begins investigating family history at the archives. While brief, this represents a shift toward resolution and family understanding.

actBreaks.description: Final confrontation in family house begins

validation.ruling: Scene #181 serves as both climax and Act 3 break - Gustav's moment of artistic commitment and acceptance of mortality effectively ends the main dramatic arc before the resolution.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Circular narrative that weaves between multiple time periods, using the house as both setting and narrative device. The structure mirrors memory itself - non-linear, associative, and layered with meaning.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Deliberate, contemplative pacing with time jumps creating emotional rhythm. Extended sequences in Deauville and family interactions balanced against brief, poetic historical vignettes. The house sequences serve as breathing spaces between more intense character confrontations.

incitingIncident.description: Nora enters to find her mother Sissel dead in bed, a winter coat on a nearby chair suggesting someone recently left. She gently touches her mother's hand and forehead before Agnes appears in the doorway.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. NURSING HOME / MOTHER'S ROOM - EVENING

validation.ruling: Scene #39 is a textbook inciting incident - Nora discovers her mother has died, which sets the entire story in motion. This moment of loss catalyzes all subsequent events including the family reunion, Gustav's return, and Nora's emotional journey. The placement at 10.1% is perfectly positioned within the acceptable range.

midpoint.description: Erik's 9th birthday party at Agnes and Even's apartment. Gustav arrives with energy and charm, immediately winning over Erik and bonding with Nora over teasing Even, while Agnes observes their alliance with mixed feelings.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APA RTMENT - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #119 functions as an effective midpoint at 57.2%. Erik's birthday party serves as a pivotal family gathering where Gustav's charismatic but problematic nature is on full display. This scene shifts relationship dynamics and sets up the third act conflicts around Gustav's film project and family relationships.

climax.description: Gustav confesses to Michael his loss of creative control and exhaustion with filmmaking, admitting he may need to replace Erik and questioning his ability to continue at his age

validation.ruling: Scene #181 at 93.5% serves as the climax where Gustav confronts his artistic mortality and decides to commit fully to his final film project. This represents the peak dramatic moment where the central character makes his defining choice, perfectly positioned in the climax window.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (PRESENT, WINTER) - DAY
scenes.summary: The family house in Oslo stands weathered and paint-worn in winter, possessing a soulful quality that newer, better-maintained neighboring houses lack.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (APPROX. 2000, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: The same house in summer 2000, freshly painted and vibrant. Nora's childhood essay assignment to write as an object led her to choose their house, its windows and door resembling a face.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1990S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Two girls thunder down the stairs, grab school bags and rush out, slamming the door. Nora's essay described how the house's belly shook as she and her sister ran through the back door.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1990S) - DAY
scenes.summary: The house watches through multiple windows as the two girls run through the garden, squeezing through a fence hole to take their shortcut to school until they disappear from view.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (VARIOUS TIMES) - DAY
scenes.summary: Montage of the house enduring decades of use: high heels scraping parquet at parties, dog claws scratching floors, chairs dragging, children's dirty fingers on stair railings.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1990S) - DAY
scenes.summary: A noticeable crack mars the house's foundation, an old flaw discovered shortly after the house was built a hundred years ago, according to Nora's father.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1990S) - DAY
scenes.summary: The foundation crack extends through the basement and up through the ground floor library, visible all the way to the ceiling, suggesting structural instability.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (APPROX. 2000)
scenes.summary: Twelve-year-old Nora covers her head with a pillow to block out her parents' loud argument. Her essay described the house as slowly collapsing, with their family's time just a split second in mid-air.
scenes.turningPointNote: Establishes core family dysfunction and Nora's perspective on generational trauma

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1918, FALL) - DAY
scenes.summary: In 1918, Nora's great-grandfather Edvard Irgens lies dead from Spanish flu in the second-floor bedroom, flowers covering his body in the room where his granddaughter would later be born.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1925, WINT ER) - DAY
scenes.summary: Seven years later in 1925, the same bedroom witnesses new life as a midwife places newborn Edith in her mother's arms, the cycle of life continuing in the same space.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (APPROX. 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: In the same bedroom 75 years later, Nora's parents Sissel and Gustav engage in a fierce but muted argument, the sound distant and dreamlike.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (CA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: Back in her childhood room, young Nora presses a pillow over her head, the fabric muffling her parents' ongoing argument from the bedroom above.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1955, SPRING) - DAY
scenes.summary: In 1955, Otto drops an orange that rolls away due to the slanted floor. He notices something is wrong and freezes before dying of a heart attack, his body hitting the floor.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1958) - DAY
scenes.summary: In 1958, Otto's daughter Karin sits in the living room with her head down. The narrator reveals she hanged herself in the room at the end of the hallway at age 36.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals the pattern of tragic deaths and suicide in the family lineage

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1985) - DAY
scenes.summary: Karin's sister Edith died thirty years later in 1985, in the same bedroom where she was born, completing another generational cycle of birth and death.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (2008) - DAY
scenes.summary: In 2008, Nora's childhood room serves as storage but retains her bed. Her teenage essay avoided the word 'quarrel,' describing her parents' fighting as 'noise' the house disliked.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (QUICK MONTAGE, VARIOUS YEARS & SEASONS)
scenes.summary: Through the house's window-eyes, Gustav is seen leaving repeatedly across different years, his departures becoming more frequent and stressed as time passes.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (APPROX. 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: Sissel sits melancholically in the kitchen while young Nora watches. The house missed Gustav's sounds after he left for good, preferring noise to the oppressive silence that followed.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marks the father's abandonment and its impact on the family dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE, NORA’S CHILDHOOD ROOM (2008) - DAY
scenes.summary: Twenty-year-old Nora reads her old essay in the cluttered childhood room, finding it disappointingly unemotional. She chose Nina's monologue from The Seagull for her Theatre Academy auditions instead.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora, now 35, stands in darkness at the back of Norway's National Theatre stage, breathing nervously as she faces a full auditorium of expectant theatre guests.
scenes.turningPointNote: Establishes Nora's current crisis and her struggle with performance anxiety

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL THEATRE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Documentary-style footage shows audience members arriving at the National Theatre premiere, snow covering the square and statues of famous playwrights Ibsen, Bjørnson, and Holberg.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The theatre lobby bustles with activity as patrons check coats, collect programs, and stream into the stately hall filled with empty red seats awaiting the performance.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, MAIN HALL & BEHIND STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hundreds of expectant guests fill the theatre as the performance is about to begin. Nora hides in total darkness at the back of the stage, frantically breathing and scared.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, DRESSING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora has retreated to a sparse dressing room, clearly panicking. The stage manager pounds on the door demanding her presence, but Nora refuses to come out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Peak of Nora's performance anxiety crisis, complete breakdown before the show

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, AUDITORIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The stage manager pushes through the full auditorium to alert the director that something is wrong. The director notices the commotion and gets up to handle the crisis.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, DRESSING ROOMS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The director tries to convince panicked Nora to perform, telling her this happens every time and always works out. Nora insists this time is different and she cannot do it.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora is finally miked up by the sound technician after the director successfully persuades her. The irritated stage manager is assured that everything will be fine once Nora gets on stage.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, AUDITORIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The director returns to his seat next to his husband, smiling reassuringly as the house lights begin to dim and the audience murmur slowly fades to expectant silence.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora stands with other actors ready to go on stage, trying to breathe calmly but failing. She makes eye contact with Jakob and waves him over as she backs away toward the hallway exit.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora sprints down the hallway in her large costume as the stage manager chases her. She begins tearing at her hair and costume until the costume supervisor and Jakob intervene.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora's complete meltdown and attempt to destroy her costume and escape

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, M AIN AUDITORIUM & BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Confusion spreads through the darkened auditorium as nothing happens on stage. Backstage, Nora is physically restrained by a stage technician while crew frantically repairs her costume with duct tape and fixes her makeup.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, DRESSING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora's empty dressing room contains only her abandoned phone, which starts vibrating with an incoming call that goes unanswered.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE / BACKSTAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora exits the stage with other actors after a successful performance. Everyone hugs and smiles as she receives a bouquet of flowers, which she graciously gives to the stage technician who had restrained her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Resolution of the performance crisis, showing Nora did eventually perform successfully

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, DRESS ING ROOM
scenes.summary: Back in her dressing room, Nora's phone continues vibrating alarmingly with urgent calls that she has not yet seen or answered.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora sits stressed in a taxi, having changed to civilian clothes and removing her stage makeup with wet wipes, clearly in a hurry to get somewhere important.

scenes.heading: EXT. NURSING HOME PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The taxi arrives at a large hospital-like building in a nursing home parking lot, suggesting the urgent nature of Nora's destination.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI IN THE NURSING HOME PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora has her payment ready but remains seated in the taxi with her hand on the door handle, asking the concerned driver if she can just sit there for a moment before going in.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSING HOME - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora walks through nursing home corridors, finding the specific room she's looking for and slowing down as she approaches the door.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSING HOME / MOTHER'S ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora enters to find her mother Sissel dead in bed, a winter coat on a nearby chair suggesting someone recently left. She gently touches her mother's hand and forehead before Agnes appears in the doorway.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery of the mother's death, the central loss that drives the entire narrative

scenes.heading: EXT. THE ROAD OUTSIDE THE FAMILY HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: A taxi drops off Gustav, Nora's father, in front of the family house. The well-known but past-his-prime film director looks tired and creased, having a distinctly bad day as he faces the house.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE/KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes and Nora prepare food in the kitchen for their mother's funeral reception. Nora suggests they should have ordered catering, while Agnes focuses on the task, both dressed elegantly in black.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOME, LIVING ROOM/ KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav enters the house filled with funeral guests and surveys his old home. He spots Nora but retreats to the empty library before she notices him, the room bearing traces of his deceased wife's psychology practice.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: The house suddenly becomes completely deserted and quiet, all the funeral guests vanishing as faint music from far away begins to play, creating a dreamlike transition.
scenes.turningPointNote: Magical realism moment transitioning from present to past, beginning extended flashback sequence

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1930S, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: The same library in the 1930s, now sunny and vibrant. Twelve-year-old Karin plays music on a hand-cranked record player with friends, having claimed the rarely-used room for her gatherings.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1930S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Music travels through the fireplace to ten-year-old Edith's room above, decorated with 1930s movie star photos. She lies by the fireplace listening to Karin's music and friends' laughter.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1940S) - EVENING
scenes.summary: A decade later in the 1940s, seventeen-year-old Edith listens through the fireplace to indistinct excited discussion below, but the music is turned up to drown out the voices.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1940S, FALL) - DAY
scenes.summary: From a first-floor window, twenty-year-old Karin is seen walking out the gate in her coat, suggesting a departure or journey.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1943, WINTER) - EVENING
scenes.summary: In winter 1943, Karin is escorted from the house by Nazi-uniformed State Police to a waiting car. As she's put in the back seat, she casts a final glance up toward the second floor.
scenes.turningPointNote: Karin's arrest by Nazi collaborators, revealing the family's resistance activities during WWII

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1 944, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: After Karin's release six months later in summer 1944, her room is found empty one morning. She had fled to Sweden, leaving behind most of her belongings on the shelves.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1944, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: The first-floor library sits abandoned in summer 1944, the record player unused. Without Karin, the room that once filled the house with music and laughter remains silent and empty.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (PRESENT DAY, WINTER) - DAY
scenes.summary: During the funeral reception, Gustav sits thoughtfully while the narrator reveals Karin's return to Norway as Karin Borg after the war, with family photographs showing her journey from youth to wife and mother.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1950S, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: A joyful montage of 3-year-old Gustav in the 1950s family house - running through rooms, playing with spilled milk, standing on the porch in rain - establishing his deep childhood connection to the home.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1950S, VARIOUS SEASONS) - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav's childhood love for the house continues through beautiful imagery of sunlight creating magical shadows and reflections throughout the rooms across various seasons, showing the house as a place of wonder.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1958) - DAY
scenes.summary: The house darkens as Karin sits in the dim living room before walking to the end hallway room where she dies, with Gustav taken to Sweden by Lennart - a pivotal loss of home and mother.
scenes.turningPointNote: Karin's death separates Gustav from his beloved childhood home, establishing the central trauma

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1965) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Years later, Aunt Edith and her maid Lillian transform the house into a vibrant party venue with liberated 1960s guests, loud music and dancing - the house reborn with joy and life.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1965) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: External view of the house with all first floor windows lit up during Edith's full party, showing the home's transformation into a place of celebration and community.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1965) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: From inside the party, neighbors turn on lights in response to the noise, with Edith suspecting one neighbor had betrayed her sister Karin during the war - past trauma surfacing.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 1965, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: Edith gives teenage Gustav bold life advice about embracing pleasure with anyone willing, while he watches Lillian sunbathing - a formative moment of sexual awakening and liberation philosophy.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 1965, SUMMER) - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Edith and Lillian sit together in intimate silence on the steps at sunset, with the narrator revealing Gustav will inherit the house when Edith dies - establishing future ownership.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1986, FALL) - DAY
scenes.summary: The house stands empty and worn down by 1986, having lost its 1960s charm and vibrancy, awaiting Gustav's return to restore it.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1986, FALL) - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav at 35 paints walls in the second floor bedroom, having moved back to Norway and the house with beautiful light - reclaiming his inheritance and childhood home.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1986, FALL) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav sits motionless amid painting equipment, showing his mother's melancholy as he looks toward the room where she died, triggering psychological issues that lead him to therapist Sissel.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2000) - EVENING
scenes.summary: A TV interview shows confident 50-year-old Gustav telling his romantic version of meeting Sissel, contrasting with the narrator's revelation that he was an unmotivated therapy patient.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (EARLY 90S) - DAY
scenes.summary: 3-year-old Nora runs joyfully through the modernized, brighter house in the early 90s as the narrator describes Gustav and Sissel's quick marriage and growing family.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (MONTAGE, 1988 - 2020)
scenes.summary: A layered montage of family memories from 1988-2020 showing tender moments like Gustav showing baby Nora the moon, contrasted with later scenes of plates shattering and conflict.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2020) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The house at night with lights extinguishing one by one until only one window remains lit, showing the family's gradual departure and abandonment of the once-lively home.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2020) - DAY
scenes.summary: Signs of Sissel's deteriorating mental state - items misplaced in the fridge, reminder notes everywhere, a melted kettle, and adult Agnes comforting her crying mother in the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (PRESENT DAY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Back at the funeral reception, Nora leads young Erik toward the hallway, explaining that Grandma Sissel used to work in her office helping people before she got sick.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE/STAIRS TO 2ND FLOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Erik walk upstairs as she explains how Sissel talked to people about secret things in her therapy practice, sharing the intimate nature of her mother's work.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: In Nora's cluttered former bedroom, she shows Erik the old fireplace where she used to secretly listen after school, hearing Gustav's clear Swedish voice from below.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora discovers her father Gustav has unexpectedly arrived at the funeral reception, creating awkward family reunions and nervous energy as she and Agnes approach him by the fireplace.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: As the funeral reception winds down, Gustav brings in large speakers through the back door, suggesting he plans to stay and make himself at home again.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The sisters clean up alone, drinking wine and discussing their father's return, with Agnes revealing Gustav technically still owns the house despite giving it to Sissel during divorce.
scenes.turningPointNote: The house ownership revelation creates immediate stakes for Nora's security and future

scenes.heading: INT. LORRY PUB - DAY
scenes.summary: At Lorry Pub, Gustav and Nora have their first real conversation, with him expressing concern for her while flirting inappropriately with the server and discussing his life in France and Sweden.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CAR IN DEAUVILLE, FRANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: In Deauville, France, Gustav arrives with nervous festival journalist Christophe, learning that his old contact Jean-Baptiste died of mouth cancer four years ago.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONGRESS CENTER, DEAUVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Deauville film festival, Gustav signs autographs for a few fans while major stars like Rachel Kemp draw the real crowds and attention.

scenes.heading: INT. CONGRESS C ENTER, DEAUVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav and Christophe walk past the main cinema toward a smaller venue, with Gustav observing the young star Rachel Kemp being surrounded by fans before her American film screening.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALLER CINEMA, DEAUVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: On stage at the smaller cinema, Gustav introduces his film retrospective with self-deprecating humor about nudity and low viewership, showing his defensive wit about his limited success.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. TRAIN COMPARTMENT 40S - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav's new film plays, showing young Agnes as Anna running from Nazi soldiers toward a train, with the boy (based on Karin's story) stumbling behind - family trauma transformed into art.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL CINEMA HALL, DEAUVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: After the film screening, Rachel Kemp sits with tears in her eyes while Gustav appears melancholic and vulnerable during the Q&A, his cocky facade stripped away by his own work.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gustav confronts the emotional weight of his family trauma through his art

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH PROMENADE IN DEAUVILLE - JUST BEFORE SUNSET
scenes.summary: Gustav walks alone along the beach promenade at sunset, passing festival banners featuring Agnes as Anna, lost in melancholic thoughts about his film and family history.

scenes.heading: EXT. FANCY FRENCH BRASSERIE BY THE BEACH - EVENING
scenes.summary: Outside a fancy French brasserie at evening, paparazzi and fans wait behind barriers hoping to glimpse Rachel Kemp, while Gustav approaches the restaurant.

scenes.heading: INT. FANCY FRENCH BRASSERIE BY THE BEACH - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rachel Kemp recognizes Gustav at the restaurant and invites him to join her table, praising his film as a masterpiece while her entourage makes space for him.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEAUVILLE BEACH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav, Rachel and her manager walk on the beach at night, enjoying champagne and the freedom of 'sneaking away' from the festival obligations and public attention.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEAUVILLE BEACH - DA WN
scenes.summary: At dawn on the beach, Gustav and Rachel walk intimately in the sand discussing her desire to take a break from projects that don't connect to who she is as a person.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEAUVILLE BEACH (2024, SP) - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: As sunrise breaks over the water, Rachel opens up about her absent father's lack of interest in her, while Gustav compliments her expressive face and natural acting ability.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN’S APARTMENT - EVENING
scenes.summary: In Agnes and Even's apartment, they watch Gustav's documentary about Edvald, a dying blue frog described as the last of his species, while questioning why it's only in English.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT, ERIK’S ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora tucks Erik into bed in his room, sharing tender moments about love and his innocent proposal to marry her when he grows up, showing their special bond.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the living room, Agnes and Even notice Nora has tears in her eyes after the sweet bedtime moment, revealing her emotional vulnerability beneath the surface joy.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora returns alone to her practical but cold apartment, sitting in semi-darkness at the kitchen table in the quiet emptiness, highlighting her isolation and loneliness.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora walks to work, entering the National Theatre through the side entrance, continuing her professional routine despite her emotional struggles.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Theatre Director confronts Nora about her performance anxiety affecting other actors, but also mentions that director Olivia has requested her for a lead role in a new production.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTM ENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and married actor Jakob lie in bed after sex, discussing therapy as she reveals she avoids serious relationships by choosing unavailable men to avoid confronting her dysfunction.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: A flashback to 20 years ago shows the closed door of Sissel's therapy office with faint conversation heard from inside, establishing the history of secrets within the house.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE/ SISSEL'S OFFICE (CIRCA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: Inside Sissel's office during therapy sessions, different clients discuss relationships, bathroom isolation, and fear of commitment, showing the intimate work conducted in the family home.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE/ NORA'S CHILDHOOD ROOM (CIRCA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: 12-year-old Nora secretly listens through the fireplace in her childhood room to her mother's therapy sessions, hearing clients discuss relationships and Stockholm syndrome, absorbing adult psychological concepts.
scenes.turningPointNote: Young Nora's eavesdropping on therapy sessions shapes her understanding of relationships and dysfunction

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ REHEARSAL SPACE (PRESENT DAY) - DAY
scenes.summary: At theatre rehearsal, Nora analyzes her character by comparing her to Agnes, revealing how their father's abandonment affected her sister's relationship choices and fear of criticism.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - EVENING
scenes.summary: The rehearsal conversation continues at Agnes and Even's apartment, with Agnes encouraging Nora to focus on what she loves about acting rather than what costs her emotionally.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ REHEARSAL SPACE - DAY
scenes.summary: After rehearsal, Nora and Jakob share intimate looks and wait for others to leave before being alone together, showing their secretive affair and her attraction to unavailable men.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: In flashback, 12-year-old Nora returns from school to find Gustav conducting a read-through of his film ANNA with young Agnes and other actors in the kitchen, showing his use of family in his art.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS IN OSLO - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora walks through Oslo streets as her voiceover reflects on acting as escape from self. Visually, she's replaced by different women - a 50-year-old, young woman with yoga mat, career woman - as Agnes asks why she likes not being herself.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora sits alone in her bedroom, reading and making notes in her manuscript, isolated in contemplation.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUR SAVIOR'S CEMETERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora jogs intensely through the green cemetery, seeking solace among the graves and memories.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora sits alone in darkness, her face lit by phone screen as she laughs at something, soft sounds echoing in the empty apartment highlighting her solitude.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS IN OSLO - DAY
scenes.summary: The woman with the red bike pauses while locking her bike, tears in her eyes, representing universal female loneliness.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: The 50-year-old woman sits at Nora's kitchen table in semi-darkness, embodying pressing loneliness. Camera approaches as other women replace her until it's Nora sitting there, suggesting shared female isolation.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora approaches the family house in summer, garden green. Agnes stands on terrace. They hug and smile, Nora appearing happy and unaffected.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: In the family house, Agnes shows Nora their mother's packed belongings. Agnes mentions wanting to move in when mother got sick. She pleads with Nora to take things with sentimental value, like a vase, but Nora seems detached from material memories.
scenes.turningPointNote: Central conflict over sentimental value and family legacy emerges as Nora rejects connection to mother's belongings

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora hurries out through the back way, taking the childhood shortcut through the fence hole, still holding the vase - connecting past and present.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav opens the door for Rachel but blocks his agent and Nicky, directing them to a café while Rachel stays for private family time.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav introduces Rachel to Agnes in the family house. Rachel admires the home, learns Agnes is a historian, and discovers Agnes played Anna in Gustav's earlier film, establishing generational artistic connections.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEDIEVAL CASTLE (TV SERIES SHOOTING) - DAY
scenes.summary: Michael, excited Danish producer at medieval castle film set, calls Gustav with news that 'she's in' - likely referring to Rachel's casting commitment.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav sits confidently trimming toenails while Michael celebrates on speakerphone. New Order plays as Michael mocks Gustav's old music taste, but Gustav turns it up louder.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Rachel Kemp fans gather outside downtown Oslo hotel with posters and hearts, hoping to glimpse their idol.

scenes.heading: INT. DOWNTOWN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Hotel press day with Gustav, Rachel, and other actors. Gustav insists on working with Peter, his longtime cinematographer, despite Michael's concerns about Peter's age and decade-long retirement.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora eats cereal while watching phone coverage of Gustav's press day, hearing him swear and call someone an idiot in Swedish.

scenes.heading: INT. TUBE STATION (2024, SU) - DAY
scenes.summary: In tube station, Nora encounters Rachel Kemp's face on all digital billboards. They seem to look directly at each other, creating unsettling connection between Nora and her replacement.

scenes.heading: EXT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora approaches Agnes's apartment building carrying a gift, appearing nervous as she rings the doorbell for Erik's birthday.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APA RTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Erik's 9th birthday party at Agnes and Even's apartment. Gustav arrives with energy and charm, immediately winning over Erik and bonding with Nora over teasing Even, while Agnes observes their alliance with mixed feelings.
scenes.turningPointNote: Family dynamics shift as Gustav and Nora reconnect, excluding Agnes despite her efforts to bring family together

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT/ ERIK'S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Erik works on PC using AI to create something based on Gustav Borg film style, checking DVD titles for reference - young generation engaging with grandfather's artistic legacy through technology.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT/ LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav warns against Erik becoming screen-obsessed like 'pale guys who can't talk to women,' targeting Even who's on his phone. Gustav and Nora bond over mocking Even while Agnes tries to engage her husband.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT/ BALCONY - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav and Nora smoke on balcony in light mood. Gustav mentions Therese held his hand, Nora laughingly warns him not to try sleeping with her.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - EVENING
scenes.summary: Evening with leftover cake and calvados. Gustav lectures Nora about writing her own material instead of waiting for acting roles. He mentions Rachel starting her own production company, emphasizing control over artistic destiny.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS IN OSLO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora walks pensively through deserted Oslo streets at night, lone car passing, emphasizing her isolation after family gathering.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTHER STREETS IN OSLO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav walks alone on different Oslo streets looking troubled, parallel to Nora's solitary journey.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav enters dark family house, turns on small hallway lamp, sits exhausted to remove shoes but remains seated as car lights sweep through room.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora returns home, opens refrigerator in dark kitchen, sits with beer as Agnes calls but she lets it ring unanswered, choosing isolation over connection.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora sits on tram looking tired, continuing her journey through emotional numbness.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ REHEARSAL SPACE - DAY
scenes.summary: Alone on National Theatre stage, Nora looks into dark auditorium then breaks down crying desperately, falling to knees then floor in raw emotional collapse witnessed by unseen colleagues.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora's emotional breakdown represents rock bottom, witnessed by others, potentially career-damaging vulnerability

scenes.heading: INT. CAR SWEDISH COUNTRYSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav drives through Swedish countryside reminiscent of landscapes in his film 'Anna,' connecting present journey to past artistic work.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEAUTIFUL HOUSE BY THE SWEDISH COAST - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav visits Peter at beautiful coastal house. Peter sleeps under parasol with Gustav's script on his lap. They reunite warmly, Peter signaling Gustav to sit in adjacent lounger overlooking breathtaking sea view.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAUTIFUL HOUSE BY THE SWEDISH COAST - DAY
scenes.summary: In kitchen, Gustav watches Peter's shaking hands pour whiskey. Peter insists he's fine once eye meets viewfinder, but Gustav realizes Michael was right - Peter is too old to work.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gustav confronts reality that his longtime collaborator can no longer work, threatening his film and artistic legacy

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav drinks alone in family house, affected by Peter meeting. Gets drunk, notices kitchen door frame marked with Nora and Agnes's childhood height measurements over the years.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Drunk Gustav sits on bed frame in empty master bedroom where he once lived with Sissel, melancholic about lost marriage and family.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav drunkenly calls Nora, leaving rambling voicemail about missing her and struggling to communicate through her anger. Falls asleep while leaving message.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav wakes on couch fully clothed, phone by his head. Cleans up evidence of drinking binge, wiping surfaces obsessively to hide his vulnerability.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav conducts script reading with Rachel and Ingrid in family house kitchen, energized by work. Focuses on translation details and coaching Rachel on character delivery.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Garden break during script reading. Rachel questions doing film in English and accent consistency. Gustav considers her points about character motivation, particularly why the character kills herself.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN’S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Rachel visits Agnes seeking information about Gustav's mother's suicide. Agnes reveals the grandmother was imprisoned during war, which may have contributed to her death 60 years ago.
scenes.turningPointNote: War trauma connection revealed as root of generational pain, giving context to family's emotional dysfunction

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S HOTEL SUITE - EVENING
scenes.summary: In hotel suite, Rachel practices Scandinavian accent pronunciation alone, then looks at Nora's Instagram, studying the woman she's replaced in Gustav's film and life.

scenes.heading: EXT. OSLO FERRY - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora stands on ferry deck with director Olivia, Jakob, and theatre colleagues heading to island retreat.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRESSHOLMEN ISLAND - DAY
scenes.summary: On Oslo fjord island, Olivia makes toast acknowledging Jakob's home troubles. Nora surprised, later talks privately with Jakob who confirms relationship problems aren't about their affair.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRESSHOLMEN ISLAND/ON THE WAY TO THE FERRY - DAY
scenes.summary: Walking to ferry, Nora tries to kiss Jakob publicly since secrecy no longer matters, but he pulls away twice, leaving her hurt as he avoids eye contact.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jakob rejects Nora's attempt at public relationship, indicating their affair is ending

scenes.heading: EXT. OSLO FERRY - SUNSET
scenes.summary: On return ferry, everyone sits scattered and tired. Jakob talks with Olivia while Nora sits alone. Their eyes meet briefly before he looks away, confirming emotional distance.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: During script reading break, Gustav privately coaches distracted, nervous Rachel about listening and not rushing performance, suggesting they try scene without accent.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav and Ingrid lie in bed after sex, discussing how young people don't enjoy sexuality like their generation. Gustav admits the young are right about world ending, Ingrid responds that's more reason to live fully.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES - DAY
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of National Archives building, functionalist 70s architecture on Oslo's forest outskirts.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES (MONTAGE) - DAY
scenes.summary: Montage of archives' concrete mountain halls, corridors, and storage areas. Archivist moves shelves to reveal State Police documents from Main Office/Oslo Department, loading them onto trolley.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES/RECEPTION - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes receives archival documents about her grandmother Karin Irgens at the National Archives reception desk, including registration cards from State Police archives and materials from the treason trials against Ole Wilhelmsen

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES/READING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes examines her grandmother's yellowed registration card and testimony documents in the archives reading room, finding concrete proof of Karin's existence and her testimony against a torturer during WWII
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes discovers tangible evidence of her family's wartime trauma and resistance

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes processes her discovery at home with Even, reflecting through narration on her grandmother's unsentimental testimony and realizing she was one of hundreds of victims who testified against the same torturer

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - DAY - CLIP FROM FATHER'S FILM ANNA:
scenes.summary: A film clip from Gustav's movie ANNA shows a dark-haired boy fleeing through a rain-soaked forest, finding refuge by a large fairy-tale-like stone where he collapses exhausted

scenes.heading: INT. OUTDOOR AREA BY AGNES & EVEN’S APT. - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes watches her father Gustav perform a mock death scene with a plastic shovel for Erik's filming, ending with Agnes giving Gustav an unexpected small hug that surprises and pleases him

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL THEATRE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Documentary-style images show theatergoers arriving at the National Theatre for Nora's Hamlet performance, with promotional posters visible on the building

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Director Olivia sits nervously with family in the packed theater hall while Agnes and Even occupy seats with one vacancy beside them, anticipating the performance

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/DRESSING ROOMS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora prepares calmly in her dressing room, practicing tongue twisters with a cork before receiving the ten-minute call for her Hamlet performance

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/BACKSTAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora gets miked up backstage while other nervous actors watch her; she appears focused and less anxious than before, though the stage manager remains vigilant for potential problems

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/MAIN STAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav stands hidden at the back of the theater watching his daughter perform as Hamlet, becoming visibly moved before suddenly leaving the performance
scenes.turningPointNote: Gustav's emotional reaction to Nora's performance causes him to flee, suggesting deep unresolved feelings

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/FOYER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav crosses the empty theater foyer alone as sounds from the ongoing play echo faintly behind him before exiting the building entirely

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/MAIN STAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Hamlet performance concludes to thunderous applause and a standing ovation, with Nora receiving particularly enthusiastic recognition from the audience

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ACTORS' FOYER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Post-show celebration in the actors' foyer with flowers and congratulations; Agnes and Even arrive to praise Nora, but Agnes senses something is off despite Nora's apparent happiness

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/DRESSING ROOMS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora sits alone in her dressing room after the successful performance, isolated despite the celebration continuing elsewhere

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ACTORS' FOYER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora walks past the celebration room where her colleagues continue drinking and laughing late into the night, choosing not to join them

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAWN
scenes.summary: Nora sits motionless in her dark apartment at dawn, still wearing her post-performance clothes, having remained awake all night in solitude
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora's isolation after her triumph reveals her deepening depression

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora lies motionless on her living room floor, staring blankly at dust bunnies moving with her breath under the sofa

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora calls in sick to the theater from her kitchen table, her voice flat and drained of energy as clouds darken her apartment

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav arrives at Agnes's apartment in good spirits to discuss Erik's role, but Agnes confronts him angrily about missing Nora's premiere and ultimately decides to protect Erik from the film project
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes takes a stand against Gustav, prioritizing her family's wellbeing over his artistic ambitions

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - EVENING
scenes.summary: Agnes tidies her apartment feeling lighter after confronting Gustav, then discovers and becomes intrigued by his forgotten script lying on the coffee table

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes sits on the toilet reading Gustav's script, clearly captivated by the material despite her earlier rejection of the project

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes continues reading the script in bed by nightstand light while Even sleeps, finishing it in a thoughtful state

scenes.heading: EXT. NORA'S APARTMENT BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes stands outside Nora's functionalist apartment building repeatedly ringing the bell, holding Gustav's script and clearly worried about her sister's silence

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes finds Nora's apartment dark and unwelcoming with untouched takeaway food; Nora appears unkempt and tired, prompting Agnes to share Gustav's script and express concern about their family dynamics
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes confronts Nora's deteriorating state and attempts intervention through sharing their father's work

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora enters the theater through the side entrance for another day of performance despite her personal struggles

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/DRE SSING ROOMS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora prepares alone in her dressing room with conversations and noise audible outside, maintaining her professional obligations

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/MAIN STAGE AND AUDITORIUM - EVENING
scenes.summary: After another successful Hamlet performance with continued audience applause, Nora spots Rachel in the audience during the curtain call

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/FOYER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rachel waits by the stage entrance to meet Nora after the performance; they notice they're dressed similarly and share a moment of connection

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/BACKSTAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rachel and Nora move through the backstage area toward the stage, with Rachel praising the performance despite language barriers

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ MAIN STAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sitting in the empty auditorium, Rachel reveals her doubts about the film role and her struggle to understand the character, while Nora admits she can't work with Gustav due to communication problems
scenes.turningPointNote: Rachel's crisis about the role exposes the broader dysfunction in Gustav's artistic relationships

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav confesses to Michael his loss of creative control and exhaustion with filmmaking, admitting he may need to replace Erik and questioning his ability to continue at his age
scenes.turningPointNote: Gustav's vulnerable admission of artistic and personal decline marks a crucial moment of self-awareness

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rachel arrives at the family house to speak with Gustav, who welcomes her warmly despite the late hour and offers her hospitality including warm socks

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the kitchen, Rachel admits she wants to leave the film project but doesn't want to disappoint Gustav, who responds with understanding and validates her decision to prioritize artistic integrity
scenes.turningPointNote: Rachel's honest conversation with Gustav leads to mutual respect and her graceful exit from the project

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: As Gustav helps Rachel with her coat, she glances toward the room where his mother died; they share a moment about the incomprehensible nature of maternal suicide before Rachel leaves

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - MONTAGE OF DIFFERENT TIMES
scenes.summary: A dreamlike montage moves through the family house across different time periods, blending voices and memories from various eras in a ghostly overlapping effect

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav sits alone in the memory-filled house drinking whiskey, turning music up loud to drown out Michael's persistent phone calls before escaping to the cold garden without proper clothing
scenes.turningPointNote: Gustav's breakdown and exposure to the elements represents his complete emotional and physical collapse

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAWN
scenes.summary: Dawn breaks over the family house with lights still blazing and music playing loudly inside while Gustav lies motionless on the ground by the garden chair

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/ CORRI DORS - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora rushes through hospital corridors while Agnes tries to reassure her about Gustav's condition, explaining he was found very cold but conscious

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/GUSTAV’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora sits beside her sleeping father's hospital bed holding his hand, the scene deliberately echoing the film's opening after their mother's death
scenes.turningPointNote: The parallel to the opening scene completes a cycle of family crisis and care

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: At the hospital coffee machine, Agnes and Nora share a spontaneous hug while holding their coffee cups, finding comfort in each other amid the crisis

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/CORRIDOR OUTSIDE GUSTAV’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Approaching Gustav's hospital room, the sisters hear him flirting energetically with a young nurse, showing his surprisingly quick recovery and unchanged personality

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - MONTAGE SALE & RENOVATION
scenes.summary: The family house is sold and undergoes complete renovation, with all soulful details stripped away and replaced with modern, characterless white interiors
scenes.turningPointNote: The destruction of the family home represents the final erasure of generational memory and heritage

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (FILM STUDIO) - DAY
scenes.summary: In a disorienting scene, Nora makes sandwiches in the restored family kitchen which now serves as a film studio set, efficiently preparing Erik's school lunch with practiced maternal care
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that the house exists now only as a film set suggests the transformation of family history into artistic material
